summary: Ukrainian UAV groups are being launched from Russian‑occupied regions toward multiple Ukrainian oblasts, while Russian forces report civilian casualties from Ukrainian drone strikes. Ukrainian officials say up to 40% of Russian oil‑refining capacity has been knocked out by May attacks. Both sides continue air operations, including reports of Tu‑160 bomber flights and Kalibr missile launches.
key_points: Ukrainian UAV groups launched from Kursk, Bryansk, and Luhansk regions are heading toward Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63552).
A UAV group from the Black Sea is moving toward Odesa (Source: @kpszsu/63562).
Russian reports claim 70 civilians were injured in Starobilsk, with 21 fatalities (Source: @tass_agency/378497).
Ukraine says nearly 40% of Russian primary oil‑refining capacity was disabled by May, citing attacks on 15 Russian refineries including Volgograd (Source: @UkraineNow/81605).
Kalibr cruise missiles were launched from two launchers in the Caspian Sea, with up to 16 missiles fired (Source: @eRadarrua/94730).
Russian air alerts report Tu‑160 bomber activity from the Ukrainianka airfield and potential Tu‑95MS/Tu‑160/Tu‑22M3 sorties overnight (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67338, @mon1tor_ua/67334).
Multiple Shahed attacks were reported over Kharkiv, Odesa, and the Black Sea area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67318).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV groups are being dispatched from Russian‑occupied territories toward Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia, indicating intensified drone operations. Russia reports significant civilian casualties from Ukrainian drone strikes and confirms Kalibr missile launches from the Caspian Sea. Ukraine claims up to 40% of Russian oil‑refining capacity has been disrupted by May attacks.

points: Ukrainian UAV groups originated in Kursk, Bryansk, and Luhansk regions of Russia and are targeting Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, showing coordinated cross‑border drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/63552).
A separate UAV formation from the Black Sea is heading toward Odesa, suggesting a focus on southern coastal targets (Source: @kpszsu/63562).
Ukrainian forces claim to have struck 15 Russian oil refineries between January and May, disabling roughly 40% of Russia's primary oil‑processing capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81605).
Ukraine reports that Russian air defenses have intercepted multiple Shahed loitering munitions over Kharkiv and the Black Sea corridor toward Odesa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67318).
Russian officials report 70 injured civilians, including 21 deaths, in Starobilsk after a Ukrainian strike (Source: @tass_agency/378497).
Russia states that Ukrainian attacks used UAVs produced by Ukraine and NATO members (Source: @tass_agency/378499).
Kalibr cruise missiles were launched from two launchers in the Caspian Sea, with up to 16 missiles fired, indicating long‑range strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/94730).
Russian media claim a Ukrainian drone hit a civilian car in Shchekino, Kursk region, causing a civilian death (Source: @tass_agency/378515).
UAV groups are moving along the northern Chernihiv border toward Kyiv region, indicating pressure on the northeastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/63561).
Multiple UAV groups reported heading toward Sumy, Romny, and Terney, suggesting sustained activity on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/63561).
A new reactive UAV is reported operating north of Chernihiv toward Snovsk (Source: @eRadarrua/94720).
Ukrainian air alerts note Shahed attacks over Kharkiv and the surrounding area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67337).
UAVs from the north of Chernihiv are heading toward Kyiv region, following the Belarus border (Source: @kpszsu/63561).
A UAV group from the north of Chernihiv is reported moving toward the city of Chernihiv itself (Source: @kpszsu/63514).
Eight civilians, including three children, were injured in a Russian attack on Snovske, Chernihiv region (Source: @UkraineNow/81587).
Russian reports of a civilian death from a Ukrainian drone in Shchekino, Kursk region, highlight cross‑border threats (Source: @tass_agency/378515).
UAV groups from the south are targeting Mykolaiv and Zaporizhzhia, indicating pressure on the southern axis (Source: @kpszsu/63557).
A UAV from the south is reported heading toward Velyka Oleksandrivka, a location in the south (Source: @kpszsu/63515).
Russian attacks on a maternity ward in Odesa caused damage but no casualties, showing continued strikes on southern infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81599).
Kalibr missile launches from the Caspian Sea could reach southern Ukrainian targets (Source: @eRadarrua/94730).
Tu‑160 bomber activity was reported from the Ukrainianka airfield, indicating potential strategic bomber sorties (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67338).
Nighttime alerts warn of possible Tu‑95MS, Tu‑160, and Tu‑22M3 bomber launches, as well as Kalibr missile and MiG‑31K fighter activity (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67334).
Multiple Shahed loitering munitions were observed over Kharkiv, Odesa, and the Black Sea area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67318).
Ukrainian air defense reported a false alarm at 22:22 in Kyiv, showing high alert levels (Source: @eRadarrua/94691).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAV groups launched from Kursk, Bryansk, and Luhansk regions are targeting Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63552).
A UAV formation from the Black Sea is moving toward Odesa, indicating a focus on southern coastal targets (Source: @kpszsu/63562).
Russia reports 70 civilian injuries and 21 deaths in Starobilsk after a Ukrainian strike (Source: @tass_agency/378497).
Ukraine claims up to 40% of Russian primary oil‑refining capacity was disabled by May attacks, including the Volgograd refinery (Source: @UkraineNow/81605).
Kalibr cruise missiles were launched from two launchers in the Caspian Sea, with up to 16 missiles fired (Source: @eRadarrua/94730).
Tu‑160 bomber activity was reported from the Ukrainianka airfield, suggesting possible strategic bomber missions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67338).
Multiple Shahed attacks were reported over Kharkiv, Odesa, and the Black Sea corridor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67318).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian drone operations are expanding from multiple launch points in occupied territories, increasing pressure on both northern and southern fronts. Russian civilian casualties and infrastructure damage indicate that Ukrainian strikes are achieving tactical effects, while Russia's Kalibr missile launches and strategic bomber activity suggest a continued long‑range strike capability. The high volume of Shahed loitering munitions poses a persistent air defense challenge for Ukrainian forces.
sections.regional_response: Western officials have highlighted the impact of Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil refining, noting significant reductions in processing capacity. Russian statements emphasize civilian harm from Ukrainian drone strikes, aiming to shape international perception. Both sides are maintaining heightened alert levels, with Ukraine reinforcing air defenses and Russia continuing to publicize missile and bomber deployments.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch sites in Kursk, Bryansk, and Luhansk is critical for anticipating future strike vectors. The reported Kalibr launches from the Caspian Sea warrant tracking of missile trajectories toward Ukrainian coastal and inland targets. Continued observation of Shahed activity will inform air defense resource allocation across the eastern and southern sectors.
